+    def test_parsing_rfc850_two_digit_year_sliding_window(self):
+        # RFC 7231: two-digit years more than 50 years in the future should
+        # be interpreted as the most recent year in the past with same digits.
+        current_year = datetime.now().year
+        # Year 0 should be in 2000 (within 50-year window from any current year)
+        parsed = parse_http_date('Monday, 01-Jan-00 00:00:00 GMT')
+        self.assertEqual(
+            datetime.utcfromtimestamp(parsed).year,
+            2000,
+        )
+        # Year 99: sliding window determines century
+        # 99 < (current_year % 100 + 50) → 2099, else 1999
+        parsed = parse_http_date('Monday, 01-Jan-99 00:00:00 GMT')
+        expected_year = 2099 if 99 < (current_year % 100 + 50) else 1999
+        self.assertEqual(
+            datetime.utcfromtimestamp(parsed).year,
+            expected_year,
+        )
+        # Year in the past (e.g., 94) should resolve correctly
+        parsed = parse_http_date('Sunday, 06-Nov-94 08:49:37 GMT')
+        expected_year = 2094 if 94 < (current_year % 100 + 50) else 1994
+        self.assertEqual(
+            datetime.utcfromtimestamp(parsed).year,
+            expected_year,
+        )
+

+    assert list(pages[0]) == []

+def test_paginator_iter_single_page():

+def test_paginator_iter_matches_page_range():
+    """Test that iteration yields same pages as page_range approach."""
+    items = list(range(1, 26))  # 25 items
+    paginator = Paginator(items, per_page=5)
+

+    old_pages = [paginator.page(num) for num in paginator.page_range]
+    
+    # New way using __iter__
+    new_pages = list(paginator)
+

+"""
+Test for the LaTeX inline code highlighting whitespace fix.
+
+Issue: #10251 enhancement adds whitespace at start and end of inline code in PDF output.
+The fix adds '%' (LaTeX comment) to suppress trailing newlines that would otherwise
+be interpreted as spaces in LaTeX output.
+
+Before fix:
+  Inline \\sphinxcode{\\sphinxupquote{
+  \\PYG{k}{def}...
+  }} code block
+  
+After fix:
+  Inline \\sphinxcode{\\sphinxupquote{%
+  \\PYG{k}{def}...%
+  }} code block
+"""
+import pytest
+from sphinx.highlighting import PygmentsBridge
+
+# Pattern strings for assertions
+# Note: \\ in the string represents a literal backslash in the LaTeX output
+# \n represents an actual newline character
+SPHINXUPQUOTE_PERCENT = '\\sphinxupquote{%\n'  # % followed by actual newline
+SPHINXUPQUOTE_NO_PERCENT = '\\sphinxupquote{\n'  # no %, just newline (the bug)
+
+
+class TestLaTeXInlineCodeWhitespace:
+    """Test suite for LaTeX inline code highlighting whitespace fix."""
+
+    def test_highlighted_code_has_percent_at_start(self):
+        """Verify that % is added after \\sphinxupquote{ to suppress leading newline."""
+        bridge = PygmentsBridge('latex')
+        code = 'def foo(): pass'
+        hlcode = bridge.highlight_block(code, 'python')
+        
+        # Apply the fix transformation
+        hlcode = hlcode.replace(r'\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]', 
+                                r'\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{%')
+        
+        # Check that % is present right after \sphinxupquote{ (before newline)
+        assert SPHINXUPQUOTE_PERCENT in hlcode, \
+            "Missing % after \\sphinxupquote{ - will cause whitespace in LaTeX"
+
+    def test_highlighted_code_has_percent_at_end(self):
+        """Verify that % is added before closing to suppress trailing newline."""
+        bridge = PygmentsBridge('latex')
+        code = 'def foo(): pass'
+        hlcode = bridge.highlight_block(code, 'python')
+        
+        # Apply the fix transformation
+        hlcode = hlcode.replace(r'\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]', 
+                                r'\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{%')
+        hlcode = hlcode.rstrip()[:-14]  # strip \end{Verbatim}
+        hlcode = hlcode.rstrip()  # strip trailing newline  
+        hlcode += '%\n'  # add % to suppress trailing space, keep newline
+        
+        # Check that % is present before the final newline
+        assert hlcode.rstrip().endswith('%'), \
+            "Missing % at end - will cause whitespace in LaTeX"
+
+    def test_old_code_produces_whitespace(self):
+        """Verify that the old (broken) code produces unwanted whitespace."""
+        bridge = PygmentsBridge('latex')
+        code = 'def foo(): pass'
+        hlcode = bridge.highlight_block(code, 'python')
+        
+        # OLD broken transformation (without the %)
+        hlcode = hlcode.replace(r'\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]', 
+                                r'\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{')
+        hlcode = hlcode.rstrip()[:-14]  # strip \end{Verbatim}
+        
+        # The OLD code has newlines without %, causing spaces in LaTeX
+        assert SPHINXUPQUOTE_NO_PERCENT in hlcode, \
+            "Old code should have newline without % (demonstrating the bug)"
+        assert hlcode.endswith('\n'), \
+            "Old code should end with newline without %"
+
+    def test_full_inline_code_output(self):
+        """Test the complete inline code output matches expected format."""
+        bridge = PygmentsBridge('latex')
+        code = 'def foo(1 + 2): pass'
+        hlcode = bridge.highlight_block(code, 'python')
+        
+        # Apply the fix transformation
+        hlcode = hlcode.replace(r'\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]', 
+                                r'\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{%')
+        hlcode = hlcode.rstrip()[:-14]  # strip \end{Verbatim}
+        hlcode = hlcode.rstrip()  # strip trailing newline  
+        hlcode += '%\n'  # add % to suppress trailing space, keep newline
+        
+        full_output = 'Inline ' + hlcode + '}} code'
+        
+        # Verify the complete structure
+        assert '\\sphinxcode{\\sphinxupquote{%\n' in full_output
+        assert '%\n}}' in full_output
+
+    def test_multi_line_code(self):
+        """Test that multi-line code also gets proper % treatment."""
+        bridge = PygmentsBridge('latex')
+        code = 'if True:\n    pass'
+        hlcode = bridge.highlight_block(code, 'python')
+        
+        # Apply the fix transformation
+        hlcode = hlcode.replace(r'\begin{Verbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]', 
+                                r'\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{%')
+        hlcode = hlcode.rstrip()[:-14]  # strip \end{Verbatim}
+        hlcode = hlcode.rstrip()  # strip trailing newline  
+        hlcode += '%\n'  # add % to suppress trailing space, keep newline
+        
+        # Verify % at start and end
+        assert SPHINXUPQUOTE_PERCENT in hlcode
+        assert hlcode.rstrip().endswith('%')

+def test_catalog_message_locations_no_duplicates():
+    """Test that Catalog.__iter__ deduplicates message locations.
+
+    Regression test for issue where duplicate locations appear in .pot files
+    when the same message appears at the same source:line multiple times.
+    """
+    from sphinx.builders.gettext import Catalog, MsgOrigin
+
+    catalog = Catalog()
+    msg = 'Test message'
+
+    # Add same message at same location multiple times
+    origin1 = MsgOrigin('/path/to/file.rst', 10)
+    origin2 = MsgOrigin('/path/to/file.rst', 10)  # Same location
+    origin3 = MsgOrigin('/path/to/file.rst', 20)  # Different location
+    origin4 = MsgOrigin('/path/to/file.rst', 10)  # Same as origin1
+    origin5 = MsgOrigin('/other/file.rst', 5)     # Different file
+
+    catalog.add(msg, origin1)
+    catalog.add(msg, origin2)
+    catalog.add(msg, origin3)
+    catalog.add(msg, origin4)
+    catalog.add(msg, origin5)
+
+    messages = list(catalog)
+    assert len(messages) == 1
+    message = messages[0]
+
+    # Should have 3 unique locations
+    assert len(message.locations) == 3, f'Expected 3 unique locations, got {len(message.locations)}'
+
+    # No duplicates in locations

+    assert len(location_set) == len(message.locations), 'Duplicate locations found'

+@pytest.mark.sphinx('dummy', testroot='domain-py-field-xref')
+def test_field_xref_currentmodule(app, status, warning):
+    """Test that :type: and :rtype: fields respect py:currentmodule context."""
+    app.builder.build_all()
+
+    doctree = app.env.get_doctree('index')
+    refnodes = list(doctree.traverse(pending_xref))
+
+    # Find xrefs for 'A' target and check their py:module context
+    # The xrefs should respect the currentmodule directive
+    a_xrefs = [n for n in refnodes if n.get('reftarget') == 'A']
+
+    # There should be 6 xrefs for 'A' (3 :param: and 3 :rtype: for each of g() and h())
+    # from functions g() and h() with py:currentmodule context
+    # f() also has no 'A' xrefs since they use fully qualified names
+    assert len(a_xrefs) == 6, f"Expected 6 xrefs for 'A', got {len(a_xrefs)}"
+
+    # First 3 'A' xrefs should be from function g() with py:module='mod'
+    for xref in a_xrefs[:3]:
+        assert xref.get('py:module') == 'mod', \
+            f"Expected py:module='mod', got {xref.get('py:module')}"
+
+    # Last 3 'A' xrefs should be from function h() with py:module='mod.submod'
+    for xref in a_xrefs[3:]:
+        assert xref.get('py:module') == 'mod.submod', \
+            f"Expected py:module='mod.submod', got {xref.get('py:module')}"
+
+    # There should be NO "more than one target found" warnings
+    assert 'more than one target found for cross-reference' not in warning.getvalue()

+def test_find_obj_class_attribute_priority():
+    """Test that class attributes are found before global variables with same name.
+
+    This is a regression test for the issue where instance variables would incorrectly
+    link to global variables of the same name. When a classname is provided in the
+    ref context, class attributes should be prioritized over global variables.
+    """
+    env = Mock(domaindata={})
+    domain = PythonDomain(env)
+
+    # Set up both a global variable and a class attribute with the same name
+    domain.note_object('somename', 'data', 'somename_id')
+    domain.note_object('Foo.somename', 'attribute', 'Foo_somename_id')
+
+    # Test 1: When classname is provided, class attribute should be found first
+    matches = domain.find_obj(env, None, 'Foo', 'somename', 'attr', searchmode=0)
+    assert len(matches) == 1
+    assert matches[0][0] == 'Foo.somename', \
+        f"Expected Foo.somename but got {matches[0][0]}"
+
+    # Test 2: Without classname, global variable should be found
+    matches = domain.find_obj(env, None, None, 'somename', 'data', searchmode=0)
+    assert len(matches) == 1
+    assert matches[0][0] == 'somename', \
+        f"Expected somename but got {matches[0][0]}"
+
+    # Test 3: With modname and classname, most specific match should be found
+    domain2 = PythonDomain(Mock(domaindata={}))
+    domain2.note_object('mymodule.somename', 'data', 'mymodule_somename_id')
+    domain2.note_object('mymodule.Foo.somename', 'attribute', 'mymodule_Foo_somename_id')
+

+    """Test that param with dict(str, str) type is correctly rendered.

+    :param dict(str, str) opc_meta: (optional)
+

+    str) opc_meta (dict(str,) – (optional)
+    
+    Instead of the expected:
+    opc_meta (dict(str,str)) – (optional)
+    """

+            ".. py:class:: Class\n"
+            "\n"
+            "   :param dict(str, str) opc_meta: (optional) Some metadata\n"
+            "   :param List[str, int] items: list parameter\n"
+            "   :param str name: simple parameter\n")

+    doctree = restructuredtext.parse(app, text)
+    
+    # Find the field_list in the doctree

+    for node in doctree.traverse():
+        if isinstance(node, nodes.field_list):
+            field_list = node

+

+

+def isclassmethod_property(obj: Any) -> bool:
+    """Check if the object is a classmethod-wrapped property (@classmethod @property).
+    
+    This detects the pattern:
+        @classmethod
+        @property
+        def f(cls): ...
+    
+    When accessed from the class __dict__, such objects appear as classmethod
+    instances whose __func__ is a property. When accessed from the class itself,
+    they appear as bound methods whose __func__ is a property.
+    """
+    if isinstance(obj, classmethod):
+        if isinstance(obj.__func__, property):
+            return True
+    elif inspect.ismethod(obj) and isinstance(obj.__func__, property):
+        return True
+    return False
+
